free_text: Study of activation cross sections of deuteron induced
reactions on erbium in the 32-50 MeV energy range
High purity metallic Er foils.
The stack contained 10 Er foils (32um) together with
Al monitor foils (50um) and other target foils.
The stack was irradiated with 50MeV, 50nA with deuteron
beam at 2BLGLVN for 40minutes.
Four series of gamma-sepecra measurements were done.
Activity was determined with no chemical separation.
Decay data were taken from NUDAT2.6 online database.
The uncertainty of the energy scale was
estimated by taking into account the energy
uncertainty of the primary beam, the possible variation
in the target thickness and the effect of beam
straggling and cross checked through the re-measured
excitation function of monitor reaction.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the  standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters.
surface density of target nuclei(1%),
incident beam current (7%),
detector efficiency (5%)
beam loss correction max (1.5%)
statistical and peak fitting (1-20%)
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TENDL.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
